name: Art Pawn Shop
description_short: Beverly Loan Company is a luxury collateral lender specializing in fine art loans in Beverly Hills, accepting Modern and Contemporary artwork as loan collateral with valuations based on secondary market value.
description_long:
Beverly Loan Company was established in 1938 and operates as a specialized collateral lender in Beverly Hills, located in the Bank of America Building at 9440 South Santa Monica Boulevard. The company is part of Luxury Asset Capital, described as the nation's largest privately-held provider of non-bank loans using luxury assets as collateral. Their primary focus is providing an alternative to traditional financing and hasty asset sales for clients with high-value but low-liquidity assets.

The company specializes exclusively in fine art loans, accepting paintings, works on paper, prints, sculptures, and photography as collateral. They focus primarily on Modern and Contemporary art from established artists with strong secondary markets, maintaining a comprehensive list of accepted artists ranging from historical figures like Picasso and Kandinsky to contemporary artists like Takashi Murakami and Njideka Akunyili Crosby. Loans are based on professional valuation of artwork provenance, requiring documentation such as auction receipts, gallery invoices, or catalogue raisonné details. Clients receive initial loan offers through pre-evaluation before scheduling appointments.

Beverly Loan distinguishes itself through its institutional-grade security infrastructure and expedited funding process. Assets are stored in a vault-within-a-vault at the Bank of America with 24-hour surveillance. The company emphasizes speed (funding in minutes), confidentiality, and fair market valuations conducted by staff trained at the Gemological Institute of America. Clients meet in private offices and receive full loan amounts in cash with no upfront fees. Loans have a four-month term with renewal options available.

This service is narrowly tailored to high-net-worth individuals with significant fine art collections seeking short-term liquidity without selling assets. The minimum and maximum loan amounts are not disclosed, but the focus on museum-quality artists and private vault storage suggests this targets serious collectors. The main limitation is that this is exclusively an art-focused collateral lender, not a general pawn shop, and requires extensive provenance documentation that typical pawn transactions do not require.
address: 2065 W 6th St # B11, Los Angeles, CA 90057

company_info.headquarters: 2065 W 6th St # B11, Los Angeles, CA 90057

pros: Specializes in fine art valuation with staff trained at the Gemological Institute of America, ensuring fair secondary market-based assessments
Offers same-day cash funding with no upfront fees or prepayment penalties
Maintains institutional-grade security with vault-within-vault storage and 24-hour surveillance in Bank of America
Provides private, confidential transactions in dedicated offices without invasive personal financial inquiries
Accepts works from 100+ established Modern and Contemporary artists with proven secondary market liquidity
Four-month loan terms with automatic renewal options, providing flexibility for short-term liquidity needs
Pre-evaluation and initial loan offers provided before appointment scheduling, streamlining the process
cons: Requires extensive provenance documentation (auction receipts, gallery invoices, catalogue raisonné) that not all artworks possess
Limited to Modern and Contemporary fine art only—does not accept other luxury collateral like jewelry, watches, or vehicles
No published loan-to-value ratios or interest rates disclosed on website, requiring direct contact for pricing transparency
Geographically limited to Beverly Hills location; no online application or remote valuation process available
Four-month minimum term may not suit borrowers seeking truly short-term liquidity or micro-loans
best_for: High-net-worth collectors with Modern or Contemporary fine art seeking short-term liquidity without selling assets
Art investors needing bridge financing between purchase and sale or awaiting auction proceeds
Collectors who value confidentiality and prefer private transactions over traditional bank financing
Estate holders or beneficiaries with significant art collections requiring rapid access to capital
diagnosis: Beverly Loan Company is best suited for affluent art collectors seeking rapid, confidential short-term financing against valuable Modern or Contemporary artworks without liquidating their collections. The primary caveat is that this is a highly specialized, geography-limited service requiring extensive provenance documentation and artwork from their approved artist list—it is not a general collateral lender and explicitly does not serve borrowers with other luxury assets or artists outside their focus area.
services: Fine art collateral loans against paintings, drawings, and works on paper
Sculpture and three-dimensional artwork collateral loans
Professional artwork valuation based on secondary market analysis
Provenance documentation review and authentication support
Pre-evaluation and initial loan offer estimation
Cash disbursement in minutes upon loan agreement
Secure vault storage with 24-hour surveillance and insurance
Pledge agreement preparation and loan administration
Four-month loan terms with renewal options
Private office consultations with Client Engagement team
Expert staff consultation on accepted artist markets
